# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Kubernetes Deployment manifests with Istio sidecar injection disabled using sidecar.istio.io/inject:"false" annotation. Skip sidecar proxies for certain services that need direct network access without mesh policies. This is for services that require low latency without proxy overhead.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app # replace with your app's actual name or id, e.g., 'my-nginx', etc...
spec:
  replicas: 3   # adjust this to the number of pod instances you want for high availability and fault tolerance (HA) in a Kubernetes cluster setup like GKE/EKS with multiple zones enabled by default or similar setups. e.g., '2' if one instance is unavailable, another suitable value depends on your app needs
  selector:   # select the pods to which these replicas should be injected (e.g.: name=my-nginx)
    matchLabels:    
      app: my-app       # replace with actual label of yours e.g., 'name':'your_service', etc...  or use "*" if you want to select all pod instances in the current namespace/cluster for this Deployment, like `"${APP_NAME}-ingress": "true",`
  template:   # define how your Pods should look (e.g.: image=nginx-1234567890) and what command to run on the container startup e.g., 'image':'myregistry/yourapp', etc...    or use one of below examples
  - name: my-pod   # replace with your pod instance id, like `"${APP_NAME}-ingress": "true",` if you want to select all the instances in a namespace for this Pod. e.g., 'name':'myapp', etc... or use one of below examples
    image: nginx   # replace with your actual docker images, like `"nginx-1234567890": "latest",` if you want to inject sidecar on top of existing pods. e.g., 'name':'myapp', etc... 
    ports:    
      - containerPort:   # adjust this based your actual needs, like `"container-port":"http","host-port":80,"protocol":"TCP",` if you want to expose the service on a specific port. e.g., 'name':'myapp', etc...  or use one of below examples
        containerPort: "8095".   # adjust this based your actual needs, like `"container-port":http,"host-port":"32467",` if you want to expose the service on a specific port. e.g., 'name':'myapp', etc...
    envFrom:     # Add environment variables for pods or use one of below examples   .e.g.: - name: "MY_ENV"  value:"dev". Use `-` if you want to add these from a ConfigMap, Secret object in the same namespace as your Deployment/Pod
      - secretKeyRef:    # provide details for all Secrets used by Pods or use one of below examples. e.g., 'name':'myapp', etc...  .e.g.:   name:"MY_SECRET", key:'env-key'. Use `"-"` if you want to add these from a ConfigMap, Secret object in the same namespace as your Deployment/Pod
        fieldRef:     # provide details for all fields used by Pods or use one of below examples. e.g., 'name':'myapp', etc...  .e.g.:   name:"MY_FIELD", path:'spec.containers[0].env[1]'. Use `"-"` if you want to add these from a ConfigMap, Secret object in the same namespace as your Deployment/Pod
    resources:     # adjust this based on how much CPU and memory are required for Pods or use one of below examples. e.g., 'limits':'{"cpu": "10","memory":"2Gi"}' if you want to set resource limits, like `name`:'myapp', etc...
    livenessProbe:   # adjust this based on how your app is supposed live and use one of below examples .e.g.:  'http-get':'{"path": "/healthz","port":"8095"}' if you want to set a probe for health checks or `name`:'myapp', etc...
    readinessProbe:   # adjust this based on how your app is supposed live and use one of below examples .e.g.:  'http-get':'{"path": "/healthz","port":"8095"}' if you want to set a probe for health checks or `name`:'myapp', etc...
---   # (--) end yaml block, add more as needed e.g., - name: "MY_CONF", value:"{'key1':'value1','kv2":"vv3"}" Use '+='-e MYVARIABLENAME=$(MYVARIABLEVALUE)' if you want to set environment variables in the pods or use one of below examples .
